title: The One with the Stoned Guy

jokes.text: Why does my cinnamon stick have an eraser?
jokes.setup: Rachel notices something odd about her cinnamon stick
jokes.punchline: It's actually a pencil with an eraser
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about a mix-up, economical and mildly amusing

jokes.text: Miss Tedlock, you're looking lovely today. May I say that is a very flattering sleeve length on you?
jokes.setup: Chandler's overly formal and oddly specific compliment
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific 'sleeve length' comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler awkwardness with perfect character voice - the sleeve length detail is wonderfully absurd

jokes.text: If this is about those prank memos, I had nothing to do with them. Nothing at all. Really. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Chandler immediately assumes he's in trouble
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top denial making him sound guilty
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's neurotic tendencies, escalating denial is funny

jokes.text: Never mind. But it was gonna be really good.
jokes.setup: Phoebe starts to organize a surprise reaction
jokes.punchline: Gives up immediately but still brags about her abandoned idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - giving up but still taking credit. Great economy and character voice

jokes.text: So I quit.
jokes.setup: Everyone congratulates Chandler on his promotion
jokes.punchline: He immediately reveals he quit instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent timing - two words that completely subvert expectations

jokes.text: Was it a lot more money? Doesn't matter. I just don't want to be a guy who sits in his office worrying about the WENUS.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains his principled stance about not wanting the job
jokes.punchline: The absurd acronym 'WENUS'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant made-up acronym that sounds dirty but isn't - highly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: 'The WENUS?' Weekly estimated net usage system. It's a processing term.
jokes.setup: Monica questions the WENUS
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan technical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the joke while maintaining Chandler's character

jokes.text: Oh, that WENUS.
jokes.setup: Chandler's explanation of WENUS
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective button on the WENUS sequence

jokes.text: I just don't have a lot of chefing experience. Unless it's an all-toast restaurant.
jokes.setup: Chandler considers the chef job
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating about only being able to make toast
jokes.explanation: Good character humor about Chandler's limited cooking skills

jokes.text: Thanks. I just don't see myself in a big white hat.
jokes.setup: Monica's enthusiasm about the chef job
jokes.punchline: Chandler focuses on the silly visual of the chef's hat
jokes.explanation: Mild visual humor about chef stereotypes

jokes.text: Can you see my nipples through this shirt?
jokes.setup: Rachel asks an awkward personal question
jokes.punchline: The blunt, inappropriate nature of the question
jokes.explanation: Typical sitcom awkward moment, gets a reaction but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No, but don't worry. I'm sure they're still there.
jokes.setup: Rachel's nipple question
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic reassurance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler comeback with great timing and delivery

jokes.text: Where you going, Mr. Suit-ey Man? I have an appointment to see Dr. Robert Pilman, career counselor-a-go-go! I added the 'a-go-go.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe's silly nickname for Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's admission that he added the nonsensical 'a-go-go'
jokes.explanation: Good character moments for both - Phoebe's randomness and Chandler's admission of silliness

jokes.text: Hey, you guys in the living room all know what you want to do.
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about not having direction
jokes.punchline: Arbitrarily excluding Ross who's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in the arbitrary distinction Chandler makes

jokes.text: I don't have a dream! The lesser known 'I Don't Have a Dream' speech.
jokes.setup: Chandler's existential crisis
jokes.punchline: Contrasting with MLK's famous speech
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of the famous speech with good self-awareness

jokes.text: Brian's Song!
jokes.setup: Monica's emotional declaration about loving her life
jokes.punchline: Joey randomly names a sad movie
jokes.explanation: Random reference humor that doesn't quite land - feels forced

jokes.text: Was it formerly owned by a blond woman and some bears?
jokes.setup: Monica describing the restaurant as 'just right'
jokes.punchline: Goldilocks and the Three Bears reference
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to fairy tale triggered by Monica's 'just right' description

jokes.text: What are you gonna make? Yummy noises. And, Monica, what are you gonna make?
jokes.setup: Monica asks what Rachel will make for dinner sounds
jokes.punchline: Chandler misunderstands and says he'll make yummy noises
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay misunderstanding that feels natural to the characters

jokes.text: You should make that thing with the stuff. You know... that thing with the stuff?
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to give cooking advice
jokes.punchline: Her completely vague non-helpful suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe unhelpfulness delivered with characteristic confidence

jokes.text: How about Tony's? If you can finish a 32-ounce steak, it's free. Anyone know a good place if you're not dating a puma?
jokes.setup: Joey suggests a restaurant with huge steaks
jokes.punchline: Ross's implication that his date eats like a wild animal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Joey's suggestion to Ross's animal comparison

jokes.text: I love you, Ross.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ross's date
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's random declaration of love
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe randomness but doesn't add much to the scene

jokes.text: He's not speaking metaphorically.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions introducing his date to his monkey
jokes.punchline: Chandler clarifies Ross literally has a monkey
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical clarification that gets the laugh without overselling

jokes.text: I tell you, that monkey's a chick magnet. She's gonna look at his furry, cute little face and it'll seal the deal.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his dating strategy
jokes.punchline: Using a monkey as a wingman
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable for Ross's character - the monkey dating strategy

jokes.text: We're talking about whipped fish. I'm just happy I'm keeping it down.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about different salmon mousses
jokes.punchline: Chandler's crude but honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Monica's fancy food talk with practical honesty

jokes.text: You're suited for data processing for a large multinational corporation.
jokes.setup: Chandler took aptitude tests to find his calling
jokes.punchline: The test recommends exactly what he already does
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic result that reinforces Chandler's quarter-life crisis

jokes.text: That's so great because you already know how to do that!
jokes.setup: Chandler's test results
jokes.punchline: Monica's obliviously optimistic response
jokes.explanation: Monica missing the point entirely makes Chandler's frustration funnier

jokes.text: You can see your nipples through this shirt.
jokes.setup: Chandler lamenting his lack of direction
jokes.punchline: Ross randomly brings up the nipple comment from earlier
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that feels forced and doesn't add to the scene

jokes.text: The French call it an amuse-bouche. Well, it is amusing.
jokes.setup: Monica explains the fancy French term
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal interpretation of 'amusing'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay on the French term

jokes.text: And I'm maintaining my amateur status so I can waitress in the Olympics.
jokes.setup: Monica hiring a 'professional waitress' over Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's ridiculous Olympics comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic applying Olympic amateur status to waitressing

jokes.text: You know, I don't mean to brag, but I waited tables at Innsbruck in '76.
jokes.setup: Rachel's Olympics waitressing joke
jokes.punchline: Taking the bit further with a specific Olympics reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that commits to the absurd premise

jokes.text: Talk dirty. Come on. Say something... hot! Vulva.
jokes.setup: Woman asking Ross to talk dirty
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward clinical word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - academic approach to dirty talk is brilliantly awkward and character-specific

jokes.text: 'Vulva?' All right, I panicked, all right? She took me by surprise. But it wasn't a total loss. I mean, we ended up cuddling.
jokes.setup: Ross's vulva comment
jokes.punchline: His defensive explanation and proud cuddling result
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that explains Ross's mindset and extends the character comedy

jokes.text: Whoa, you cuddled? How many times?
jokes.setup: Ross mentions cuddling
jokes.punchline: Joey treating cuddling like a scoring statistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - treating emotional intimacy like a sports stat

jokes.text: Come on, come on. All right, ready? Oh, Ross... you get me so hot, I want your lips on me now. See? Now you say something.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to coach Ross in dirty talk
jokes.punchline: Joey demonstrating by talking dirty to Ross himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Joey's complete lack of awareness about the awkwardness

jokes.text: Tell me you want to caress my butt! Okay, turn around. I don't want you staring at me while I do this.
jokes.setup: Joey's coaching continues
jokes.punchline: Ross's practical request to not maintain eye contact
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the awkwardness with Ross's reasonable boundary-setting

jokes.text: Say it! Say it! --run it all over your body... until you're trembling with--
jokes.setup: Ross struggling with dirty talk practice
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic coaching creating maximum awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Sustained cringe comedy building to peak awkwardness

jokes.text: It's okay. It's okay. I was always rooting for you two kids to get together.
jokes.setup: Monica walks in on Ross and Joey in compromising position
jokes.punchline: Her misunderstanding and supportive reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect button on the cringe sequence with Monica's misinterpretation

jokes.text: It's a lot less satisfying to steal pens from your own home.
jokes.setup: Chandler talking to his old boss
jokes.punchline: Admitting he steals office supplies but it's not fun at home
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about the psychology of office theft

jokes.text: Stop saying numbers! I'm telling you, you've got the wrong guy! You got the wrong guy!
jokes.setup: Boss keeps offering more money
jokes.punchline: Chandler's increasingly desperate attempts to refuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler's weakness when faced with money

jokes.text: Oh, look! That guy's peeing!
jokes.setup: Chandler showing off his office window view
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately spots something inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag deflating Chandler's office pride

jokes.text: Last time I do that. I promise.
jokes.setup: Chandler calling his secretary just to show off
jokes.punchline: His sheepish admission of the pointless power display
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Chandler's self-awareness

jokes.text: I wish I could, but I've made plans to walk around.
jokes.setup: Monica asking Rachel to waitress for more money
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic excuse about having no real plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of having absolutely no plans

jokes.text: In the cab on the way over, Steve blazed up a doobie. Smoked a joint, you know? Lit a bone. Weed, hemp, ganja.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to explain Steve is high
jokes.punchline: Her overuse of slang terms for marijuana
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel trying too hard to be cool with drug terminology

jokes.text: Okay, I'm with you, Cheech.
jokes.setup: Rachel's excessive marijuana slang
jokes.punchline: Monica's Cheech and Chong reference to shut her up
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that effectively stops Rachel's rambling

jokes.text: Well, smack my ass and call me Judy! These are fantastic!
jokes.setup: Steve tasting Monica's food while high
jokes.punchline: His bizarre enthusiastic expression
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd stoner expression that's highly quotable

jokes.text: 'Tartlets?' 'Tartlets.' Word has lost all meaning.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions tartlets
jokes.punchline: Steve's stoner fixation on repeating the word
jokes.explanation: Perfect stoner humor about semantic satiation - relatable and well-executed

jokes.text: These are-- They are like a little corn envelope, you know?
jokes.setup: Steve examining taco shells while high
jokes.punchline: His poetic description of ordinary taco shells
jokes.explanation: Good stoner observation that finds poetry in mundane objects

jokes.text: Macaroni and cheese! We gotta make this!
jokes.setup: Steve getting increasingly excited about junk food
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm for the most basic food possible
jokes.explanation: Predictable stoner behavior but executed well within character

jokes.text: Bears overboard! They're drowning! Hey, fellas! Grab onto a Sugar-O! Save yourselves! Help! Help! I'm drowning! Help!
jokes.setup: Monica pours cereal and gummy bears into milk
jokes.punchline: Steve's elaborate rescue fantasy for the candy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment showing stoner imagination at its peak - highly entertaining

jokes.text: I've waited seven years for an opportunity like this... and you can't even wait 4 1/2 minutes for a tartlet?
jokes.setup: Monica's mounting frustration with Steve
jokes.punchline: The specific timing precision amid her breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good Monica control-freak moment with perfect timing specificity

jokes.text: I was the James Michener of dirty talk. It was the most elaborate filth you have ever heard. I mean, there were characters, plot lines... themes, a motif. At one point, there were villagers.
jokes.setup: Ross describing his dirty talk success
jokes.punchline: Treating it like an academic literary work with villagers
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Ross moment - perfectly captures his academic approach to everything, escalating to absurd villagers detail. Highly quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Yes, Fran, I know what time it is... but I'm looking at the WENUS and I'm not happy!
jokes.setup: Chandler working late at his new job
jokes.punchline: Using the WENUS terminology seriously now
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Chandler has become what he mocked - now seriously invested in the WENUS

jokes.text: Oh, the throb!
jokes.setup: Phoebe giving someone a massage
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate exclamation during the massage
jokes.explanation: Mildly suggestive Phoebe moment that gets a reaction but isn't particularly clever

tmdb_overview: Monica cooks for a restaurateur prospect who is intoxicated; Chandler receives a promotion; Ross dates a colleague.
